originalTitle: Wall St posts record closing highs despite pause in AI rally
summary: STORY: All three of Wall Street's main indexes notched record closing highs on Wednesday despite muted gains. The Dow climbed the most, adding more than a third of a percent, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq ticked up marginally. Rising healthcare and consumer stocks boosted the Dow, while investors took a pause from the AI rally. :: Archive And bank stocks slid after JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned that expenses this year for the lender could be $1 billion higher than estimated. Still, Jason Betz, private wealth advisor for Ameriprise Financial, points to financial stocks as primed for growth due in part to the benefits of AI. "Where I really like the opportunity is where companies are going to be able to leverage more AI, not to cut jobs, but leverage AI to move the needle on employee productivity and push bottom line. I think financials, which have had a tough go of it, have opportunity, number one, because they are implementing more AI technologies, and number two, I think it's possible that the market is maybe sleeping on the fact that we're going to see, in all likelihood, big asset management fee revenue come in here over the coming months for these financial companies." Among other movers, shares of Zscaler tumbled more than 31% after the cloud security firm projected fourth-quarter revenue below expectations. Shares of Bath & Body Works jumped almost 10% after the retailer reported first-quarter sales and profit above expectations, while Abercrombie & Fitch advanced nearly 9% after posting a strong quarterly profit. :: Marvell Technology And shares of Marvell Technology, down 4.5% at the close, rose in extended trading after the company forecast quarterly revenue above estimates, signaling strong demand for its networking and custom silicon chips used in AI data center buildout. :: Archive Also on Wednesday, Goldman Sachs raised its 2026 year-end forecast for the S&P 500 to 8,000 from 7,600, citing continued strength in corporate earnings.

The Dow climbed the most, adding more than a third of a percent, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq ticked up marginally. Rising healthcare and consumer stocks boosted the Dow, while investors took a pause from the AI rally. :: Archive And bank stocks slid after JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned that expenses this year for the lender could be $1 billion higher than estimated. Still, Jason Betz, private wealth advisor for Ameriprise Financial, points to financial stocks as primed for growth due in part to the benefits of AI. "Where I really like the opportunity is where companies are going to be able to leverage more AI, not to cut jobs, but leverage AI to move the needle on employee productivity and push bottom line. I think financials, which have had a tough go of it, have opportunity, number one, because they are implementing more AI technologies, and number two, I think it's possible that the market is maybe sleeping on the fact that we're going to see, in all likelihood, big asset management fee revenue come in here over the coming months for these financial companies." Among other movers, shares of Zscaler tumbled more than 31% after the cloud security firm projected fourth-quarter revenue below expectations. Shares of Bath & Body Works jumped almost 10% after the retailer reported first-quarter sales and profit above expectations, while Abercrombie & Fitch advanced nearly 9% after posting a strong quarterly profit. :: Marvell Technology And shares of Marvell Technology, down 4.5% at the close, rose in extended trading after the company forecast quarterly revenue above estimates, signaling strong demand for its networking and custom silicon chips used in AI data center buildout. :: Archive Also on Wednesday, Goldman Sachs raised its 2026 year-end forecast for the S&P 500 to 8,000 from 7,600, citing continued strength in corporate earnings.
chineseTitle: 尽管 AI 涨势暂停，华尔街仍创下收盘新高
chineseSummary: 故事：尽管涨幅不大，但华尔街三大主要指数周三均创下收盘新高。道指涨幅最大，涨幅超过三分之一，而标准普尔 500 指数和纳斯达克指数小幅上涨。医疗保健和消费类股的上涨提振了道琼斯指数，而投资者则暂停了人工智能的涨势。摩根大通首席执行官杰米·戴蒙 (Jamie Dimon) 警告称，该银行今年的支出可能比预期高出 10 亿美元，此后银行股下跌。尽管如此，Ameriprise Financial 的私人财富顾问贾森·贝茨 (Jason Betz) 指出，金融股已做好了增长的准备，部分原因是人工智能的好处。 “我真正喜欢的机会是，公司能够利用更多的人工智能，不是裁员，而是利用人工智能来提高员工的生产力并推动利润。我认为，经历了艰难的金融行业有机会，第一，因为他们正在实施更多的人工智能技术，第二，我认为市场可能正在沉睡，因为我们很可能会在未来几个月看到这些金融公司的大量资产管理费收入。”在其他推动者中，云安全公司 Zscaler 预计第四季度收入低于预期，其股价下跌超过 31%。 Bath & Body Works 公布第一季度销售额和利润超出预期后，其股价上涨近 10%，而 Abercrombie & Fitch 公布强劲的季度利润后，股价上涨近 9%。 Marvell Technology 股票收盘下跌 4.5%，但在该公司预测季度收入高于预期后，该公司股价在盘后交易中上涨，表明对其用于人工智能开发的网络和定制硅芯片的强劲需求
chineseContent: 故事：尽管涨幅不大，但华尔街三大主要指数周三均创下收盘新高。道指涨幅最大，涨幅超过三分之一，而标准普尔 500 指数和纳斯达克指数小幅上涨。医疗保健和消费类股的上涨提振了道琼斯指数，而投资者则暂停了人工智能的涨势。摩根大通首席执行官杰米·戴蒙 (Jamie Dimon) 警告称，该银行今年的支出可能比预期高出 10 亿美元，此后银行股下跌。尽管如此，Ameriprise Financial 的私人财富顾问贾森·贝茨 (Jason Betz) 指出，金融股已做好了增长的准备，部分原因是人工智能的好处。 “我真正喜欢的机会是，公司能够利用更多的人工智能，不是裁员，而是利用人工智能来提高员工的生产力并推动利润。我认为，经历了艰难的金融行业有机会，第一，因为他们正在实施更多的人工智能技术，第二，我认为市场可能正在沉睡，因为我们很可能会在未来几个月看到这些金融公司的大量资产管理费收入。”在其他推动者中，云安全公司 Zscaler 预计第四季度收入低于预期，其股价下跌超过 31%。 Bath & Body Works 公布第一季度销售额和利润超出预期后，其股价上涨近 10%，而 Abercrombie & Fitch 公布强劲的季度利润后，股价上涨近 9%。 Marvell Technology 股票收盘下跌 4.5%，但在该公司预测季度收入高于预期后，该公司股价在盘后交易中上涨，表明对其用于人工智能开发的网络和定制硅芯片的强劲需求
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
